body:
Diff contains only an execution result.json artifact; no sessions.spec.ts test was added or modified. Acceptance criteria require a Playwright e2e test for evidence artifact detail view, which is absent from the changed files.
harness=claude
model=opus
input_bytes=3279
output_bytes=774
elapsed_ms=10845

post-merge review: BLOCK (flagged for human)
Diff contains only an execution result.json artifact; no sessions.spec.ts test was added or modified. Acceptance criteria require a Playwright e2e test for evidence artifact detail view, which is absent from the changed files.
result_rev=d1b1144b20b96cadefc4b43673e5b1dca6b8c724
base_rev=3894a324c4243f202f4aeeffd35fca0763dfe3e1

Bead ddx-d1c4e27a — no changes this attempt.

What is done:
  The acceptance-criteria test already exists in the codebase at
  cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/sessions.spec.ts (lines 289-415):
  test('expanded session links to per-run evidence artifact detail view', ...).

  It was added and merged in a prior successful attempt:
    commit dcb18d44 — "test(e2e): add per-run evidence artifact detail test [ddx-d1c4e27a]"
    merged via b9feaa89 — "Merge bead ddx-d1c4e27a attempt 20260501T035638- into main".

  The test navigates to the sessions list, expands the latest session row,
  follows the Execution link to /executions/<id>, and asserts the per-run
  evidence artifact detail view renders manifest/prompt/result tabs with
  bundle paths and bodies. This satisfies the AC verbatim:
    "navigates to a run/session, verifies evidence artifact detail view
     is accessible".

What is blocking:
  Nothing technically blocks closure — the work is already merged. The
  bead was re-queued because the most recent attempt's diff (from base
  checkpoint 8c8e7521) contained only execution-evidence artifacts and
  no sessions.spec.ts edits, which the reviewer interpreted as missing
  the test. The test is in fact present at base because it was merged
  in a prior attempt (dcb18d44).

What a follow-up would need:
  Either (a) close this bead manually, recognizing the test is already
  on main, or (b) update the review tooling so it inspects HEAD's
  sessions.spec.ts for the named test rather than only the per-attempt
  diff. Re-adding the test in this attempt would create a duplicate
  and is not the right action.

Verification pointer:
  git show dcb18d44:cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/sessions.spec.ts
  grep -n "per-run evidence artifact detail view" \
    cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/sessions.spec.ts
